intro: The most concrete split between Alter and Black is the pricing model: Alter runs on custom pricing only (no public tier), while Black uses free and open-source. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Alter carries low lock-in (proprietary agent identity platform with custom protocols) and custom pricing only (no public tier); Black carries low lock-in (oss python formatter) and free and open-source. Alter is a fit when you are mobile. Black is a fit when you are mobile. The honest trade-off: Alter is explicitly not for hobby projects; Black is explicitly not for hobby projects. On the product side, Alter is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Agent Identity, and Black is described as Security Scanning / Mobile App Sec tool for developers. Specializes in Python Formatter.
quick_take: Alter is for mobile; Black is for mobile; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Alter if its custom pricing model fits your budget shape better than Black's free model, and you are mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Black if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches mobile.

faq.a: Headline yes, Black is free and open-source while Alter uses custom pricing only (no public tier). But the total cost depends on self-hosting and maintenance effort for Black versus managed convenience from Alter. Factor in both.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Alter to Black?
faq.a: Migration from Alter to Black is plausible because Alter has low lock-in (proprietary agent identity platform with custom protocols). You would still have to map config, permissions and integrations to Black's model. Plan for dual-run and rollback.

On our scoring Black rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Alter. That suggests Black feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Black a good alternative to Alter?
faq.a: Black targets the same best-fit audience as Alter (mobile). Note that Black is explicitly not for hobby projects, so it only replaces Alter if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (custom vs free), which may shift total cost.
